---
title: "How to close pay gaps"
output: html_document
date: "2025-12-18"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


## Step 2: Load Data
```{r}
# Load employee data
df <- read.csv('../data/eu_company_pay_data.csv')

# Check structure
str(df)

# View first few rows
head(df)
```


## Step 3: Prepare Variables
```{r}
# Calculate full-time equivalent total pay
df$total_pay_fte <- df$total_pay / df$fte

# Create log-transformed pay (for percentage interpretation)
df$log_pay <- log(df$total_pay_fte)
```

## Step 4: Set reference values
```{r}
# Set Male as reference level for gender
df$gender <- relevel(factor(df$gender), ref = "Male")

# Determine reference grade by minority count (best gender balance)
tab <- with(df, table(grade, gender)) 
minority_counts <- pmin(tab[, "Male"], tab[, "Female"])
ref_grade <- names(minority_counts)[which.max(minority_counts)]

# Set grade factor with chosen baseline 
df$grade <- relevel(factor(df$grade), ref = ref_grade) 

# Convert other categorical variables to factors 
df$job_family <- as.factor(df$job_family) 

# Verify reference levels 
cat("Gender reference level:", levels(df$gender)[1], "\n") 
cat("Grade reference level:", levels(df$grade)[1], "\n")
```

```{r}
calculate_pay_gap <- function(fit) {
  # Extract all coefficients into a named vector
  coefs <- coef(fit)
  
  # Main gender effect (reference grade)
  beta_F <- coefs["genderFemale"]
  
  # Identify all grade-specific interaction names
  int_names <- grep("^genderFemale:grade", names(coefs), value = TRUE)
  
  # Subset those interactions; R will give NA for any dropped terms
  beta_ints <- coefs[int_names]
  
  # Reference grade gap
  gap_ref <- 1 - exp(beta_F)
  
  # Other grades: apply interaction adjustment
  gaps <- sapply(int_names, function(nm) {
    beta_int <- coefs[nm]
    if (is.na(beta_int)) {
      return(NA)
    }
    1 - exp(beta_F + beta_int)
  })
  
  # Extract grade names from interaction terms
  grade_names <- gsub("^genderFemale:grade", "", int_names)
  
  # Build results data frame
  result <- data.frame(
    Grade = c(ref_grade, grade_names),
    PayGap_Percent = round(100 * c(gap_ref, gaps), 1),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  return(result)
}
```

```{r}
target_gap <- 5
fit <- lm(log_pay ~ gender * grade + seniority + job_family, data = df)
initial_pay_gap <- calculate_pay_gap(fit)
residuals <- fit$residuals
```

```{r}
gmin <- rep(-10, nrow(initial_pay_gap))
gmax <- rep(10, nrow(initial_pay_gap))
gamma <- gmin
targets <- sign(initial_pay_gap$PayGap_Percent) * target_gap
```

```{r}
for (j in 1:100) {
  df$new_log_pay <- df$log_pay
  for (i in 1:nrow(initial_pay_gap)) {
    gender_in_scope <- if (initial_pay_gap$PayGap_Percent[i] > 0) {
      "Female" 
    } else "Male"
    idx <- df$grade == initial_pay_gap$Grade[i] & df$gender == gender_in_scope
    df$new_log_pay[idx] <- df$new_log_pay[idx] + pmax(residuals[idx], gamma[i]) - residuals[idx]
  }
  
  new_fit <- lm(new_log_pay ~ gender * grade + seniority + job_family, data = df)
  new_pay_gap <- calculate_pay_gap(new_fit)
  distance <- sign(initial_pay_gap$PayGap_Percent) * (new_pay_gap$PayGap_Percent - targets)
  
  overshot <- distance < 0
  undershot <- distance > 0
  gmax[overshot] <- gamma[overshot]
  gmin[undershot] <- gamma[undershot]
  
  gamma <- (gmin + gmax) / 2
}
```

```{r}
new_pay_gap
```


```{r}
df$raise <- round(exp(df$new_log_pay) - df$total_pay_fte, 2)
df$new_total_pay_fte <- df$total_pay_fte + df$raise

# Cost to close
sum(df$raise * df$fte)

# Number of raises
sum(df$raise>0)
```
